panels.description: Actual vs Target PPM per machine over time. A machine consistently running below its TargetPPM is a capacity constraint.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "manufacturing")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "opcua")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.run_id =~ /^${run_id}$/)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field =~ /^M\d+_ActualPPM$/)
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: last, createEmpty: false)
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value", "_field"])

from(bucket: "manufacturing")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "opcua")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.run_id =~ /^${run_id}$/)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field =~ /^M\d+_TargetPPM$/)
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: last, createEmpty: false)
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value", "_field"])

panels.title: Actual vs Target PPM per Machine

panels.description: Cumulative seconds each machine spent Blocked (downstream congested) or Starved (upstream empty). High BlockedTime on machine N means machine N+1 is the bottleneck. High StarvedTime on machine N means machine N-1 is the bottleneck.

targets.query:
import "strings"

from(bucket: "manufacturing")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "opcua")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.run_id =~ /^${run_id}$/)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field =~ /^M\d+_(BlockedTime|StarvedTime)$/)
  |> last()
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({
      machine: strings.trimSuffix(v: strings.trimSuffix(v: r._field, suffix: "_BlockedTime"), suffix: "_StarvedTime"),
      metric: if strings.hasSuffix(v: r._field, suffix: "_BlockedTime") then "BlockedTime" else "StarvedTime",
      _value: r._value
  }))
  |> group(columns: ["machine"])
  |> pivot(rowKey: ["machine"], columnKey: ["metric"], valueColumn: "_value")

panels.title: Blocked vs Starved Time per Machine (seconds)

panels.description: Machine utilisation % over time. The machine with consistently highest utilisation is likely the bottleneck. Corroborate with buffer levels — a buffer that fills before a machine confirms the constraint.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "manufacturing")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "opcua")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.run_id =~ /^${run_id}$/)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field =~ /^M\d+_Utilisation$/)
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: last, createEmpty: false)
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({r with _value: r._value * 100.0}))
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value", "_field"])

panels.title: Machine Utilisation Over Time (%)

panels.description: Overall Equipment Effectiveness (%) per machine at the end of the selected time range. World-class benchmark is 85%+.

targets.query:
import "strings"

from(bucket: "manufacturing")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "opcua")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.run_id =~ /^${run_id}$/)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field =~ /^M\d+_OEE$/)
  |> last()
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({machine: strings.trimSuffix(v: r._field, suffix: "_OEE"), OEE: r._value * 100.0}))
  |> keep(columns: ["machine", "OEE"])

panels.title: OEE per Machine (%)

panels.description: OEE loss breakdown per machine: Availability (unplanned downtime), Performance (speed losses), Quality (defect yield). All shown as % at the end of the selected range.

targets.query:
import "strings"

from(bucket: "manufacturing")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "opcua")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.run_id =~ /^${run_id}$/)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field =~ /^M\d+_(Availability|Performance|Quality)$/)
  |> last()
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({
      machine: strings.trimSuffix(v: strings.trimSuffix(v: strings.trimSuffix(v: r._field, suffix: "_Availability"), suffix: "_Performance"), suffix: "_Quality"),
      metric: if strings.hasSuffix(v: r._field, suffix: "_Availability") then "Availability"
              else if strings.hasSuffix(v: r._field, suffix: "_Performance") then "Performance"
              else "Quality",
      _value: r._value * 100.0
  }))
  |> group(columns: ["machine"])
  |> pivot(rowKey: ["machine"], columnKey: ["metric"], valueColumn: "_value")

panels.title: OEE Components per Machine (Availability / Performance / Quality %)

panels.description: Buffer fill levels (parts) over time. A buffer that stays full indicates the downstream machine cannot keep up. An empty buffer indicates the upstream machine cannot keep up.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "manufacturing")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "opcua")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.run_id =~ /^${run_id}$/)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field =~ /^B\d+_Level$/)
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: last, createEmpty: false)
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value", "_field"])

panels.title: Buffer Levels Over Time (parts)

panels.description: Cumulative good vs defective parts per machine at the end of the selected range. Machines with high defect counts are quality loss points that reduce downstream yield.

targets.query:
import "strings"

from(bucket: "manufacturing")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "opcua")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.run_id =~ /^${run_id}$/)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field =~ /^M\d+_(GoodParts|DefectParts)$/)
  |> last()
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({
      machine: strings.trimSuffix(v: strings.trimSuffix(v: r._field, suffix: "_GoodParts"), suffix: "_DefectParts"),
      metric: if strings.hasSuffix(v: r._field, suffix: "_GoodParts") then "GoodParts" else "DefectParts",
      _value: r._value
  }))
  |> group(columns: ["machine"])
  |> pivot(rowKey: ["machine"], columnKey: ["metric"], valueColumn: "_value")

panels.title: Good vs Defective Parts per Machine

panels.description: Cumulative part count output per machine over time. The machine with the lowest output relative to its line position is the production constraint.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "manufacturing")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "opcua")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.run_id =~ /^${run_id}$/)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field =~ /^M\d+_PartCount$/)
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: last, createEmpty: false)
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value", "_field"])

panels.title: Cumulative Part Count per Machine
